candidates.stmt:
Altrincham is a great place to live and work – it’s where I’ve chosen to raise my family, in part because of the fantastic schools we have locally. In recent years though, our area has been let down by the Labour Council. We’ve seen pressure on school places locally grow into a crisis. Now some families near me are even having to send their children to school miles away in Stretford or Partington just to find a place. Safety concerns around the Cresta Court hotel being used to house a significant number of male asylum seekers continue to be ignored. And our roads are the worst I’ve ever seen. Yet the Council Tax we pay has gone through the roof, with Trafford Labour having put bills up by an extra £721 since 2019, for a Band D home.

Altrincham deserves better. That’s why I’ve decided to stand up for the area I call home and fight for our schools and for our town to get its fair share.

I’m Chris Halliday, a data analyst and local resident in Ashton upon Mersey, and I’m proud to be part of the Conservative team holding this failing Labour council to account.

Labour’s Ashton councillors are backing plans to build on Goodwood Field, against the wishes of local residents. I will oppose any plans to build on this vital green space.

Labour have lost control of Trafford’s finances, raising council tax by 7.5% two years in a row, above the referendum threshold, while bringing back green bin charges.
At the same time, we have some of the worst potholes in Greater Manchester, blocked drains and missed bins. We’ll address this decline with a clear plan to focus on the basics and get spending under control.

Trafford has fantastic schools, but too many children are being sent to places far away from where they live, putting parents and children under pressure. I’ll fight for a new secondary school in South Trafford and help families get their children into local schools, not miles away.

Only the Conservatives have a chance to get Labour out in Ashton and send a message to this failing council. If, like me, you want to see:

- Protection of our green spaces in Goodwood Field and Carrington Moss
- Council tax under control, not amongst the highest in the country
- Focusing on getting the basics right
- Enough local school places for local children

Vote for Chris Halliday and the Conservatives on May 7th.

For 2026 Local Election,

Cllr Dr Shengke Zhi is a sitting Bowdon councillor, charted engineer and community champion who is proud to stand up for local residents and fight for Bowdon’s future. Since being elected in 2022, he has built a strong reputation for visible local action, holding Trafford Council to account, and getting things done.

As Shadow Executive Member for Climate Change, Community and Safety, Dr Zhi focuses on the issues residents care about most: fixing roads and potholes, saving Rose Cottage, improving community safety, local school places for local children, protecting parish councils and making sure Bowdon gets the attention and a fair share of capital investment it deserves.

Dr Zhi believes politics should be about service, delivery, and accountability. He is known for working hard on behalf of residents, speaking up when Bowdon is ignored, and pushing the council for practical solutions to local problems.

Outside politics, Dr Zhi is a respected leader in the UK energy sector, with a distinguished career in nuclear, hydrogen, carbon capture and industrial decarbonisation. He is a Visiting Professor of the Royal Academy of Engineering and a Fellow of both the Institution of Engineering and Technology and the Nuclear Institute.

Dr Zhi is also widely recognised for his contribution to community life and public service. He has raised tens of thousands of pounds for charitable causes and was awarded the UK Prime Minister’s Points of Light Award in recognition of his service.

Cllr Dr Shengke Zhi is committed to protecting Bowdon, supporting local families, and delivering the strong representation residents deserve.

Paul has actively been involved in politics since 2004, serving as an elected member on Redditch Borough Council between 2014 and 2018. Paul and his family relocated to Trafford in 2020, where he's stood as a candidate in three previous elections for Reform UK. The last being for the Bucklow St Martins ward in November 2023, where he came third beating the Greens and LibDems. His career as an electrical lighting engineer has taken Paul all over the world in the construction and operation of major TV studios and cultural venues.

Jonathan is the President of the Stretford and Urmston Constituency Conservative Association and resident of Flixton. Jonathan has been involved in local politics for as long as he cares to remember. Jonathan followed in his fathers footsteps (former Flixton Ward member Cllr. Allan R. Coupe.). Jonathan previously served as a Flixton Ward councillor from 1999-2019 (Mayor of Trafford 2017/18 Trafford’s 1st 2nd generation Mayor).

“Working in and for our community has always been my passion. I continue to this very day. I’m a Governor at two Flixton Primary Schools, Trustee of The Toy House in Flixton, President of Flixton Band, Vice President of A.F.C. Urmston Meadowside and Flixton Hub Leader of The Bread and Butter Thing helping to put food on the table for people on low incomes”

In his spare time Jonathan is actively involved in Amateur Dramatics both as an actor and director. Now retired from his full time occupation, Jonathan has a part time job working as a hotel receptionist.

Paul who held office with Trafford Council as a ward councillor between 2012 – 2021 was selected by local party members and therefore has been elected as the Chairman of Stretford and Urmston Constituency Conservative Association for 2025-26.

His previous community experience includes securing National Lottery funding for the Friends of Flixton Gardens.  Helping the L&Q group, formerly THT with a community tree planting programme.

He has previously held roles as a nominated governor at Manchester University NHS Foundation Trust and also the Greater Manchester Waste Committee. Alongside serving as a LEA Governor for 12 years.

Paul is married with two teenage children and currently works as a Social Researcher.

Commenting on being elected, Paul said “I would like to thank everyone from the Association who supported me for the position.

"I would also like to pay tribute to the outgoing Chairman, Jonathan Coupe who led the association with distinction through some difficult years.

“My priorities for the Association are engaging all our local residents to help them have both an informed and robust choice of voting options. Every resident should be up-to-date with our local team and the activities we are undertaking.

"Personally, it is embarrassing that our roads and pavements are such a state of disrepair. I am insulted by the staggering increase of 7.49% Council Tax, and the avoidance of a local referendum that policy should have triggered. As well as, at that same time having a Green Bin Tax costing every household at least £45 per year.

"And therefore, a strong focus will be given to holding to account the local Labour Party and its continued failure to provide value for money for us, as residents of Trafford."

Stuart Donnelly is the Deputy Chairman Political for Stretford and Urmston Conservative Association and the Local Conservative candidate for the Gorse Hill and Cornbrook ward in the local elections to Trafford Council in 2026. Stuart has been a political campaigner for nearly 11 years and has also stood as an election candidate for Trafford Council, securing an uplift in the Conservative share of the vote in a tight marginal seat.

Stuart also has a wealth of experience in the hospitality industry, having managed bars and restaurants and has operated a bar of his own in Manchester city centre. Stuart currently works in events and operationally manages a busy events programme for a local Metropolitan local authority. Outside of politics, Stuart has raised over £20K for various charities and also chaired a Foundation which he established in memory of a late friend.

Hale has been taken for granted by the Greens for years. Whether it’s the appalling state of our roads, our blocked drains flooding streets, or our Green Councillor who has now become an MP in Manchester. Hale deserves better.

Since being elected, Cllr Natalie Shalom has worked tirelessly for residents, dealing with more than 100 individual casework enquiries since 20th November 2025 – getting potholes filled and drains unblocked.

But there is so much more to be done. Natalie can’t do it all on her own. She needs a strong team working alongside her.

You can help build a strong team in Hale by voting for me to work shoulder to shoulder with Natalie to get things done.

Your Conservative team has a strong record of being there for residents, including opposing plans to raise council tax by 7.5%, being the only party to vote against a 40% pay rise for councillors, opposing Labour’s £50 Green Bin Tax, forcing a public meeting on the Cresta Court and Ashley Hotels proposals while raising legitimate safety and community concerns, and campaigning for the basics residents expect: better roads, cleared drains, a new local secondary school, and improved local SEND provision.

Please ensure we have a strong local team standing up for Hale, making sure we get the roads fixed, drains cleared, and fight for our parks.

Rupert Kelly
Conservative Candidate for Hale

I'm Orla, your local Green Party candidate for Hale. As a resident living in the Hale ward, I know the difference that committed Green councillors make for the local community.

They can be trusted to work hard and get results for residents - making roads safer, getting dangerously damaged fences repaired, tackling flood problems and supporting community groups like the Friends of Stamford Park to help them achieve Green Flag status.

Our Green team have led the way for Trafford to be one of the first councils to declare a climate emergency, we've challenged misinformation about people seeking asylum, and we've held the council to account to update the Flood Risk Management Strategy - the first review since 2014.

By keeping the Green team in Hale together, I can continue this hard work, and be a strong voice for positive change and inclusion in Hale, speaking up for all residents.

Chris has been a Party member for more than 20 years and served as a Trafford Councillor in Brooklands for more than 10 years, and was in 2022–23 Mayor of the Borough.

Service to the Community has for almost all his life been a passion, dating back to his university days when he started a Saturday morning Youth Club for local children at his Hall of Residence. Joining a Rotary Club in 1983, motto “Service above Self”, Chris has been continuously active since at Club, District and National level.

He has been a Governor at Sale High School and at Brooklands Primary School for over 10 years and was for many years Trafford’s ‘Appointed’ Governor at the Manchester NHS Foundation Trust Hospital Group

He is a founder member of The Friends of Moor Nook Park and sits on the Sale Moor Community Centre Committee and has also been involved in the Sale West Community Hub

In his spare time Chris has been active in local sport particularly Squash at Sale Rugby Club where he is a Vice President and also at Sale Golf Club serving for 14 years as Hon Secretary and then Chairman, and as President in 2020 – 2022. He is also currently President of Sale Photographic Society.

Dr. Anna Fryer has always been interested in public service – “Growing up in Cumbria, my Mum and Dad were always encouraging us to look after others – no wonder my three other sisters also became Doctors and Teachers!” Anna’s philosophy is simple- the reason I am standing is because I have spent 20 years working in the NHS and to make patients life better, we need more people like me with real experience in health in Parliament, who understand the NHS and patient care. The last few years in the NHS and Social Care have been a failure of Government. Quite simply our citizens have found the NHS has not been there for them when they needed it most. This is not the fault of NHS workers but a choice by the Conservatives not to plan our services and staffing properly. It has been heartbreaking watching patients waiting in the Emergency Department for hours and even days. Patients face huge barriers in accessing care, be it difficulties seeing their GP or long waiting lists. This is a failure that this government should be ashamed of. A vote for the Liberal Democrats is a vote to save our NHS after years of Conservative chaos. One issue that has been raised time and again by the people of Pendle and Clitheroe on the doorsteps is the horrifying conflict in the Middle East and the humanitarian catastrophe unfolding in Gaza. Anna says “I have spoken to many people about this conflict, and the unimaginable horror facing civilians. I have been profoundly moved by our own Liberal Democrat MP Layla Moran’s work and campaigning calling for an immediate, bilateral ceasefire. As the daughter of a Palestinian she has faced the trauma of supporting her family escape yet has spoken insightfully to understand both peoples. As Liberal Democrats we have not sought to play political games and given in to the siren song of easy answers. Instead we have stood consistently and clearly for a ceasefire in Gaza and an end to the suffering of innocent civilians.

Tracey was born in Urmston and has lived in Trafford most of her life. In the autumn of 2021 she set up the Petition “no cones on Chester Road” in a direct response to resident’s frustrations caused by increased congestion and pollution in close proximity to 4 primary schools. At the time of presentation to Trafford Council, it had achieved 2,742 signatures and media coverage. Tracey felt like it was a lonely crusade to be honest through the minefield of Labour opposition yet the Conservatives cared and this experience inspired Tracey to “cross the floor” and stand as an election candidate in a hope to continue to represent residents.

Tracey has a plethora of life and work experiences. Working in the hospitality industry led to joint business’ enterprises in a catering company and cafe in Trafford, whilst simultaneously completing 2 house renovations.

Then, as a single parent to 4 young children she achieved a Bachelor of Social Science 2:1 with Honours at Manchester University, prior to a successful career as a Retail Store Manager with the Cooperative.

Tracey has in recent years, cared for young adults with mental health issues and the elderly, alongside a part time office administration role supporting her husband’s Legal business.

In addition to which, Tracey has actively assisted a local community group to “green the alleyways” securing a £6,000 grant from Groundworks.

Life for Tracey has been a bit of a rollercoaster and she expects that to continue in our current uncertain climate. Tracey sincerely wishes to aid residents in their ups and downs too.

I am a Project Manager working in Civil Nuclear & Defence, helping to keep nuclear power stations operating.  I am also a committed Christian and care deeply about the local community and its wellbeing.

I have lived in Sale most of my life and as a parent I fear for our young people’s future.  I want to see our area safe and thriving.

I am deeply concerned not only about record council tax rises and poor services, but also the proposal for a new ‘community centre with prayer room’ on the Goodwood Field.  The overwhelming feedback I have received on the doorstep is that it is not needed or wanted by the local community and they feel it is being forced upon them by Labour and the Three Meem Foundation (an Islamic charity) without either their consent or proper consultation.

I will work with anyone – Save the Goodwood Field campaign, The Conservatives, or any other group who seek to legitimately oppose the development of what many are calling a Mosque on this precious recreational green space that is in near constant use and was gifted to the community by a local Farmer many years ago.

This is not the place and now is not the time for such a development to take place in Manor ward and this project must be stopped.

If you vote for me, I give you my personal guarantee that I will do everything I can to prevent this project from going ahead on the Goodwood Field and represent your interests more effectively at the council.

"Locally, nationally and globally - The biggest threat we ALL face is that of catastrophic climate breakdown.  Locally, nationally and globally - no one is tackling this. If we ALL make small decisions locally to address this, then we can make a difference nationally and globally."

I'm standing as a candidate in Sale Central because I want to represent young people and give them a seat at the table. I have deep concerns about climate change and I am proud to stand for social justice. It would be an honour to be the first Green Party councillor in Sale Central :)

We need councils and councillors who are truly accountable to the electorate.

And we need a new kind of politics based on our national interest and on a wide and continuing public debate about what our national interest is. This way we can get government that actually represents us.

The public are fed up with tick-box consultation exercises where governments at the local and national level pretend to listen to ordinary people. And it's not good enough for politicians simply to tell us that "the science says" this or "the markets say" that so there is no alternative. We need a real political dialogue where new ideas can be contested and debated. This has to start at the local level.

In local elections, residents are concerned about potholes, waste collection, green spaces and our streets. In all of these areas, I will work to ensure that the council works with local people to support ground-up responses and actions so that residents can have real ownership of their community life.

Councillors often claim they cannot control the biggest part of their budget as it's spent on statutory services mandated by central Government. But councils look to outsource all this service provision to private companies, often owned abroad by venture capital. Why should our rising council taxes go to line the pockets of shareholders? I will campaign for more services to be provided in-house so that more of our taxes go to the local economy and councils are forced to be truly accountable for their budgets. This will not be done overnight. But even small steps will increase the knowledge available in-house, meaning that councils will be better able to negotiate with external providers and local government will build up its capacity to provide services.

Vote for me in Sale Central if you want a councillor who will work to close the gap between local government and the people it is supposed to serve.

Syeda Bushra Raza brings energy, empathy, and real-world experience to her candidacy for the Stretford and Humphrey Park ward in the 2026 local elections to Trafford Council. A proud mother of two, Syesa understands the everyday challenges facing families and is passionate about making a meaningful difference in the lives of local residents.

With a professional background in banking and customer service, Syeda has developed strong communication and problem-solving skills—qualities that make her an effective advocate for the community. Her frontline experience gives her insight into the issues that matter most to residents, from safety and support to dignity and opportunity.

Syeda’s priorities are clear:

•     Tackling crime through stronger community policing and practical prevention measures

•     Supporting the elderly and vulnerable, ensuring care and respect for those most in need

She is committed to building safer neighbourhoods and standing up for those who often go unheard. Compassionate, determined, and deeply rooted in the community, Syeda Bushra Raza is ready to be a strong local voice for Stretford.

Will Frass top priorities:

(1) Holding Amey to account

(2) Fighting for more flood protection

(3) Free parking to keep our Village centre thriving

"The global climate crisis is largely being ignored by those in power, even the air we breathe is now causing around 40,000 deaths per year.   The actions we can take locally are becoming ever more vital as idealogical austerity cuts are continually forcing bad short term decision making.  

Failed privatisation, follows failed privatisation. Unregulated public transport is fragmented and expensive.  Record amounts are spent on public services only to result in services which are in a worse state than ever before, be it education, probation or the NHS.  Amey being handed a 23 year contract for a wide range of services is absurd and  ‘One Trafford’ is anything but, as enquiries are passed on from one department to another. 

On Davyhulme park the vegetation has been decimated, 15ft high planting that has taken decades to mature, has been hacked to a foot or so off the ground and ground cover removed,  purportedly to make life easier for the cash strapped authorities to see across the park as they drive past.  The boundaries are now more porous , traffic noise has increased, air quality lowered and visual amenity has been lost.  Opportunities to create much needed wildlife habitat are missed and the open space is slowly being eroded, even  the wildlife area being under threat from being sold off.   Disparate groups pursue their own agendas , seemingly with no regard to the environment, wildlife or climate change.  Although not yet at the levels inflicted on Sheffield by Amey, many large trees throughout the borough have already been felled.

Volunteers are encouraged to fill the gaps left by de-skilling and redundancies. We are encouraged to:  weed your park, check out your own shopping, pick up the litter, clean your railway station, help out at your local hospital.  The ‘Big Society’ becomes ever more apparent as the flow of public money to corporate profits increases.

Trafford has the only green councillors in the whole of Greater Manchester and they are d

Ali Aydogdu at 21 years old is the 2026 Local Conservative Councillor candidate for the Urmston ward. Studying at Trafford college, Altrincham campus.

As a resident of Urmston I strive to improve and better our area through good communication with residents. The views and opinions of the Urmston residents is what matters the most. Ever since I joined the North Trafford Conservatives I have seen the strive and determination of our team to reach our goals and our ideal image of the area we care about. I am optimistic about the future and I look forward to helping the residents in making our area the best it can be.
